title: Physical Tricks 🏢
description: Tailgating and Baiting: Hacking the office space.

slides.title: Tailgating 🚪
slides.content:
### Follow the Leader

**Tailgating** (or Piggybacking) is when a hacker follows an authorized person into a secure building without scanning their own ID.

*   **The Trick**: Holding a box of donuts or pretending to be on a phone call so people 'politely' hold the door open for you.

slides.title: Baiting 🖱️
slides.content:
### The Forbidden USB

**Baiting** is leaving a malicious device (like a USB drive) in a public place, hoping someone will pick it up and plug it in.

*   **The Hook**: A USB labeled 'Executive Salaries 2026' or 'Secret Project Photos'.

quizData.question: You find a USB drive in the office parking lot. What is the safest action to take?

options.text: Plug it into your laptop to see who it belongs to
Take it to the IT Security department immediately
Format it and use it for your own files

quizData.explanation: Never plug unknown devices into your machine. They can contain auto-running malware that steals your data in seconds.

slides.title: Quid Pro Quo 🤝
slides.content:
### Something for Something

In a **Quid Pro Quo** attack, the hacker offers a service or a prize in exchange for information.

*   **Example**: Calling an employee and saying, "I'm from IT. We have a gift card for you if you help us test a new login portal."
